summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Notification System Phase 2. All 16 backend API tests pass (100%). Frontend UI tests confirm bell icon, notification panel, preferences toggles, and history display all work correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend server.py continues to be oversized (>2500 lines). Consider splitting notification-related code into separate module.
All notification endpoints have proper authentication guards - good security practice.
seed_data_creation: Created fresh user via /api/auth/bootstrap for clean notification testing. Test user notiftest2@kindred.app used for existing notification data validation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Notification System Phase 2 fully functional. Backend endpoints: GET /api/notifications/unread-count, POST /api/notifications/mark-read, GET /api/notifications/history (with is_read per user), GET /api/notifications/preferences (defaults for new users), PUT /api/notifications/preferences (persists changes). Frontend: Bell icon in sidebar (data-testid='notification-bell-button'), unread badge (data-testid='notification-unread-badge'), notification panel with items list, event type badges (ANNOUNCEMENT, GATHERING), mark-all-read button, view-full-history and preferences links to /settings. Settings page has notification preferences card with all 5 toggles and notification history card with read/unread indicators.
features_tested: POST /api/notifications/mark-read marks all notifications as read and returns marked_count
GET /api/notifications/history returns items with is_read field per user
GET /api/notifications/preferences returns default preferences for new users
Bell icon notification-bell-button appears in sidebar after login
Unread badge notification-unread-badge shows count when there are unread notifications
Clicking bell opens notification panel with items list
Mark all read button clears unread badge
Notification panel shows event type badges (ANNOUNCEMENT, GATHERING, etc.)
View full history link navigates to /settings
Creating an event via POST /api/events triggers a notification
Creating an announcement via POST /api/announcements triggers a notification
Settings page shows notification history items with read/unread indicators
Settings page shows notification preference toggles that can be saved
